untranslatable_reason: Signal requires Black-Scholes theoretical option valuation, IV-RV spread calculation, delta calculation, and delta-neutral hedging mechanics. These are derivatives pricing and portfolio construction concepts that cannot be expressed using only price-based technical indicators. Required inputs: theoretical option value, implied volatility, realized volatility, option delta, and hedge ratio—none of which are available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This is a volatility arbitrage signal requiring quantitative options pricing. Attempted proxy: used hvol_20 (realized volatility proxy) vs india_vix (implied volatility proxy) to approximate IV-RV spread detection, with directional entry based on price position relative to sma_50. However, this is a significant simplification that does not capture the core signal logic of selling overpriced options with delta-neutral hedges. Recommend implementing this signal in a dedicated options pricing module outside the standard backtest engine.
